source_jobs.title: Business Operations
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning, and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for members of the Business Operations team to take an active role in ensuring our success each day in power markets. Expect to wear many hats and to work on problems that are mission-critical to the business day in and day out.

In this role, you will

 - build tools and systems that power our operations,

 - drive improved efficiency and performance in our power trading unit with data-backed analysis, and

 - own projects that support new market growth and rapid scaling of our power trading unit

As an early hire in this role, you’ll have broad impact over our operations platforms, information systems, and team culture.

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.




WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE:

 - You have 4+ years of experience in operations, product, or strategy in a fast-paced environment such as a high-growth tech company; management consulting; investment banking; private equity.

 - You are highly analytical: You can take an ambiguous challenge, break it down clearly, and design a path forward. You think in frameworks without being rigid, and you move from insight to action quickly.

 - You have proficiency with Python and one or more common analytical tools such as SQL, Tableau, etc.

 - You are detail oriented: You measure twice and cut once, ensuring work products have zero defects.

 - You are an excellent communicator: As well as solving problems, you can communicate your thinking to various stakeholder audiences who may have divergent points-of-view.

 - You have a builder mentality — You thrive in 0→1 environments. You operate effectively under ambiguity, move with urgency, and are motivated by creating something new, not optimizing something that already exists. You do what it takes to move the business forward, even when the playbook hasn't been written yet.

 - You are eager to learn: You are eager to learn and grow; you enjoy the pain of a steep learning curve.




LOCATION

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco. We have a hybrid work structure, with 3 days per week in the office. Chicago is the preferred location for this role.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Quantitative Researcher for Congestion Revenue Rights
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a Quantitative Researcher for Congestion Revenue Rights to help us design, deploy, and operate autonomous, systematic strategies that realize economic value from congestion auctions, using our future information (forecasts) and energy systems models, under real-world market constraints. You’ll

 - Build a market-leading CRR book leveraging our proprietary technologies

 - Architect autonomous strategies end to end — model, forecasts, market actions

 - Work end-to-end from information development to production-ready code if resources are otherwise occupied

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.

 


WE'RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE:

 - You have 5+ years of experience applying quantitative methods to FTRs, PTP/UTC products, and/or congestion forecasting in ERCOT.

 - You have applied stochastic optimization to problems in financial or electrical engineering, operations research, or economics.

 - You have conceptual fluency with probability theory and good taste for shaping and managing distributions.

 - You have an advanced degree in quantitative finance, computer science, statistics, machine learning, operations research, or a related quantitative field.

 - You are experienced and comfortable developing and monitoring machine learning models.

 - You are a skilled programmer in Python.
   
   More importantly —
   
   - You are extraordinarily driven — you’re relentless in getting research artifacts into production and willing and capable to work end-to-end (forecasts, models, optimization, production-quality code) when supporting resources are contended.
   
   - You love winning — while you prioritize well, there’s no task too tedious if it drives performance.
   
   - You are predisposed to collaboration — we maintain a highly open and discursive environment which rewards the refinement of everyone’s ideas and requires genuine curiosity.


LOCATION

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.

THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a Quantitative Researcher for Monetization to help us design, deploy, and operate autonomous, systematic strategies that realize economic value from our future information (forecasts) and energy systems models, under real-world market constraints. You’ll

 - Identify and prioritize market opportunities by expected value and difficulty

 - Architect autonomous strategies end to end — model, forecasts, market actions

 - Optimize strategy performance by leveraging metadata to augment bidding decisions

 - work end-to-end from information development to production-ready code if resources are otherwise occupied

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.

 


WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE:

 - You have applied stochastic optimization to problems in financial or electrical engineering, operations research, or economics.

 - You have conceptual fluency with probability theory and good taste for shaping and managing distributions.

 - You have 5+ years of design, research and development experience in industry.

 - You have a deep understanding of the structure of energy or other commodity or financial markets and their dynamics.

 - You are experienced and comfortable developing and monitoring machine learning models.

 - You are a skilled programmer in Python.

More importantly —

 - You are extraordinarily driven — you’re relentless in getting research artifacts into production and willing and capable to work end-to-end (forecasts, models, optimization, production-quality code) when supporting resources are contended.

 - You love winning — while you prioritize well, there’s no task too tedious if it drives performance.

 - You are predisposed to collaboration — we maintain a highly open and discursive environment which rewards the refinement of everyone’s ideas and requires genuine curiosity.


LOCATION

source_jobs.title: Advisor — Utility Strategy (part-time)
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a part-time Advisor for our Utility Strategy to guide the design and positioning of our credit management solutions for maximum adoption by electric utilities. This is an advisory role for a seasoned former executive who has firsthand experience managing credit risk, liquidity, and collateral structures at a utility with significant renewable energy exposure.

You’ll help us bridge the gap between product innovation and utility acceptance: bringing insider knowledge of how utilities evaluate counterparties, what matters in collateral instruments, and how credit decisions are actually made. You’ll play a pivotal role in making sure our products and services speak the language of utilities, clear internal hurdles, and are positioned to become a trusted alternative to traditional credit management practices.

In this role, you will:

 - Advise on design: shape the structure and features of our collateral product so it aligns with utility credit standards and practical requirements.

 - Guide utility engagement: identify the right stakeholders and decision-makers within utilities and advise how best to approach them.

 - Stress-test acceptance: anticipate potential objections, explain what utilities really care about, and help us refine messaging to maximize credibility.

 - Leverage your network: open doors to relevant contacts across utilities and industry associations to accelerate education and adoption.

 - Be a sounding board: provide ongoing perspective on how utilities perceive credit risk, collateral, and counterparties in today’s power markets.
   


WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - You’ve been in the seat: you were a treasurer, CFO, or senior credit/risk executive at a major electric utility, with direct responsibility for managing credit exposure to counterparties and overseeing collateral instruments.

 - Deep network: you maintain strong relationships across the utility sector, including with credit, treasury, and risk decision-makers.

 - Utility mindset: you know how utilities think about credit risk, liquidity, and collateral — both formally (policies, rating agency perspectives) and informally (what actually sways internal committees).

 - Renewable exposure: bonus if you managed or oversaw a portfolio of renewable PPAs and/or owned renewable generation assets, giving you firsthand perspective on clean energy credit dynamics.

 - Practical and strategic: you can translate your experience into concrete recommendations that make our product not just conceptually sound, but operationally adoptable by utilities.

 - Trusted presence: you bring the credibility of a career in utility leadership and can help us navigate conversations at the highest levels.
   


LOCATION

This role is fully remote.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions a

source_jobs.title: Advisor — Utility Commission Strategy (part-time)
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.



🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.



THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a part-time Advisor for Utility Commission Strategy to shape how we engage state utility commissions and accelerate utility acceptance of our credit management solutions. This is an advisory role for a seasoned regulatory leader with deep experience in commission processes in states with significant renewable and storage penetration within ISO/RTO markets.

You will help us unlock the regulatory pathway to our credit management solutions adoption: clarifying how commissions influence utility credit practices, what decision-makers care about, and how to position our product so it aligns with their oversight priorities and financial risk considerations.

In this role, you will:

 - Advise on regulatory positioning: articulate how our product supports commission goals.

 - Guide regulatory engagement: refine how we communicate our value to commissions, anticipate concerns, and guide us on which stakeholders to engage and how best to approach them.

 - Translate regulatory signals: help us interpret emerging commission priorities and market developments so we can adapt our product and positioning accordingly.

 - Leverage your network: open doors with contacts across state commissions, utilities, and energy policy organizations to accelerate education and credibility.




WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - You’ve been close to the process: you served in a senior role at a state utility commission or held a regulatory affairs or policy leadership role at an electric utility or IPP, with significant exposure to commission decision-making.

 - Deep network: you maintain strong relationships across commissions and utilities, including regulatory staff, policy leaders, and utility executives who influence credit and risk decisions.

 - Regulatory mindset: you understand how commissions think about reliability, liquidity, credit exposure, and ratepayer protection.

 - Renewable exposure: you’ve worked in markets with high renewable and storage penetration.

 - Practical and strategic: you translate regulatory and utility experience into clear, actionable recommendations that make our product understandable, credible, and adoptable.

 - Trusted presence: your background gives you the credibility to help us navigate complex regulatory environments and build confidence with decision-makers.




LOCATION

This role is fully remote.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Power Market Researcher
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.



🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.



THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a Power Market Researcher to design, build, and scale analytical, forecasting, and optimization models applied to U.S. power markets. In this role, you will:

 - Build, test, and maintain forecasting model pipelines and portfolio optimization programs that help improve financial performance in power markets.

 - Analyze market outcomes and system constraints to improve model accuracy and performance.

 - Track and interpret market dynamics, including supply, demand, congestion patterns, and price formation, to inform commercial and strategic decisions.

 - Apply statistical, optimization, and (as needed) machine learning methods to identify and evaluate market opportunities.

As an early hire in this role, you will have broad impact and ownership over our analytical models, research workflows, and how markets insights are translated into decision-making across the company.

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.



WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - You have a solid understanding of U.S. power markets.

 - You have hands-on experience with at least one RTO / ISO (e.g., CAISO, PJM, ERCOT, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP).

 - You have proven experience formulating and solving optimization problems (e.g., LP, MILP, convex optimization).

 - You have a strong foundation of power systems concepts, including generation, transmission constraints, congestion, LMP formation.

 - You have strong Python skills and have use them to build and analyze quantitative models.

 - You have experience with power flow and market simulation tools such as Dayzer, Promod, PowerWorld, PSS/E, Plexos, or open-source alternatives.

 - You have a bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., electrical engineering, operations research, economics, applied mathematics, physics) and 3+ years of experience in a power market analysis or a related role.

Most Importantly:

 - You take ownership of your work, are comfortable forming and explaining your views, and can move forward effectively under ambiguity.

 - You are a lifelong learner who works well in open, collaborative environments and value constructive criticism.




LOCATION

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Originations Lead
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a project finance professional to lead the structuring and execution of transactions across renewable energy markets.

In this role, you will:

 - Own transaction execution end-to-end: Take opportunities from evaluation through structuring, negotiation, and close, with full ownership of deal outcomes.

 - Lead underwriting and structuring: Analyze transactions, build financial models, assess downside scenarios, and design structures that enable execution.

 - Build and manage relationships: Act as the primary point of contact for our client portfolio, developing durable, trust-based relationships.

 - Innovate: Develop innovative collateral structures and push the boundaries of what we can deliver across counterparties, constraints, and use cases.

 - Contribute to platform development: Act as a core user of our underwriting and origination systems, providing insights to engineering to improve workflows and AI-driven risk tools.




WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - Experienced project finance professional: You have 6+ years of experience in renewable energy project finance, analyzing, structuring, and executing complex transactions in the US.

 - Builder mentality: You thrive in 0→1 environments. You operate effectively under ambiguity, move with urgency, and do what it takes to move the business forward.

 - Creative structurer: You know how to get deals done. You are capable of designing innovative structures, working around constraints, and expanding what can be offered to clients.

 - Highly analytical: You are fluent in complex financial models and think naturally in terms of downside cases, risk allocation, and structural mitigants.

 - Strong communicator with executive presence: You explain complexity with clarity and conviction, and are comfortable engaging directly with senior counterparties and stakeholders.

 - Commercial and relationship-driven: You build strong relationships, develop trust quickly, and can position differentiated financial solutions in competitive institutional markets.

 - Team-oriented operator: You operate with high ownership and low ego, and elevate the performance of the teams around you.

 - Technology-oriented: You are excited about leveraging SALO, our proprietary, AI-powered underwriting platform that enhances your judgment and scales your impact.

 - Collateral insight: You understand how credit support is structured (e.g., letters of credit, surety bonds) in renewable energy transactions.




LOCATION

New York is the preferred location for this role.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Surety Director
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a Surety Director to lead the commercial scaling of our innovative solutions across the utility-scale renewable energy market.

In this role, you will:

 - Originate — Proactively source high-quality counterparties across IPPs, utilities, traders, corporates, and data centers. You own the relationship from first outreach through handoff to our deal team.

 - Provide market insight — Be our eyes and ears in the market. Through efficient sales feedback loops, you continuously surface what prospects care about, how competitors are positioning, and what it will take to win — feeding that intelligence directly back to our product and strategy teams to sharpen our offering.

 - Execute across commercial channels — Deploy the full range of sales and marketing tactics with the guidance of our founders, to move prospects through your funnel to close.

 - Shape our origination platform — Act as a core user and internal voice for our origination systems, partnering with engineering to translate real market dynamics into scalable workflows and AI-powered tools that increase your speed, volume, and precision.

As a key leader, you will help build the financial backbone of our future energy systems.




WE'RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - You're a proven energy surety professional — You have 7+ years of experience building surety portfolios in the renewable energy sector, with a strong and demonstrable sales track record. You know the space, the players, and the products.

 - You have a builder mentality — You thrive in 0→1 environments. You operate effectively under ambiguity, move with urgency, and are motivated by creating a new category not just optimizing an existing one. You do what it takes to move the business forward, even when the playbook hasn't been written yet.

 - You are a true sales operator — You originate. You pick up the phone. You go to conferences. You bring relationships, build new ones from scratch, and develop the kind of personal connections that convert into long-term business. You know how to position differentiated financial solutions in competitive institutional markets.

 - You communicate with anyone, at any level — You instinctively tailor your message to your audience. You can hold a crisp conversation with an analyst and a strategic one with a C-suite executive, and you know how to convey a value proposition clearly and move every conversation forward. You leave people knowing exactly what Comity does and why it matters.

 - You operate with high ownership and low ego — You are team-oriented, collaborative, and generous with context. You elevate the performance of the people around you and take accountability for outcomes without needing credit for the work.

 - You are excited about technology as a competitive edge — You see technology as a force multiplier and use it to move faster and with more precision.

 - You understand energy collateral — You are familiar with the collateral requirements that come up in PPAs and interconnection agreem

source_jobs.title: Growth Lead
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for a Growth Lead to help us scale our commercial presence across the utility-scale renewable energy market.

You will think critically about how we grow, design the strategies to get there, and then go execute them. You will work in close partnership with our founders, with a direct line to product, strategy, and engineering.

In this role, you will:

 - Design and scale our go-to-market motion — Identify the most efficient paths to growth across segments, channels, and counterparty types. Build and develop partnerships, referral networks, and origination channels that make our commercial reach repeatable and scalable.

 - Go sell — You don't just build strategies, you execute them. You go to conferences, you pick up the phone, you get in the room. You own a portion of our pipeline and are accountable for moving prospects forward.

 - Feed intelligence back into the business — Through your market interactions, you continuously surface what resonates, what doesn't, and why. You translate that into sharp, actionable feedback that sharpens our product, messaging, and targeting.

 - Shape our origination technology systems — Act as a core user of our origination tools, partnering with engineering to translate commercial realities into scalable workflows and AI-powered capabilities that multiply your reach and output as a commercial operator.

As a key early member of the commercial team, you will have a front-row seat to scaling a product, with direct exposure to our founders on growth strategy, sales, and product direction.




WE'RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE

 - You are a sharp, structured thinker — You can take an ambiguous commercial challenge, break it down clearly, and design a path forward. You think in frameworks without being rigid, and you move from insight to action quickly.

 - You have a builder mentality — You thrive in 0→1 environments. You operate effectively under ambiguity, move with urgency, and are motivated by creating something new, not optimizing something that already exists. You do what it takes to move the business forward, even when the playbook hasn't been written yet.

 - You know how to open doors — You are energized by originating new business, getting in front of people, and turning cold conversations into real opportunities. You build relationships from scratch, convey value with clarity and conviction, and follow through relentlessly.

 - You communicate with anyone, at any level — You instinctively tailor your message to your audience. You can hold a crisp conversation with an analyst and a strategic one with a C-suite executive, and you leave every conversation moving something forward.

 - You operate with high ownership and low ego — You are team-oriented, collaborative, and generous with context. You elevate the performance of the people around you and take accountability for outcomes without needing credit for the work.

 - You are excited about technology as a competitive edge — You see technology as a force multiplier and actively look for ways to use it to move faster and with more precision than the competition.

 - You have a genuine interest in energy and financial markets — You follow the energy transition closely, have a view on where power markets are

source_jobs.title: Quantitative Researcher for Risk and Research Engagement
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging state of the art statistical learning and convex optimization methods (AI) to build the financial rails of our future energy systems that will accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in complex systems, machine learning and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in New York City, Chicago, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

This role sits at the intersection of portfolio management, quantitative research, and risk management. You will partner closely with Portfolio Managers, Risk Managers, and senior leadership to develop risk models, analyze power market dynamics, improve portfolio allocation decisions, and build the analytical infrastructure that supports our trading and risk platform.

You will work directly with decision-makers across the organization to deepen our understanding of market structure, portfolio behavior, and risk drivers in U.S. power markets. This role is highly collaborative, analytical, and hands-on, with significant opportunity to shape our risk and portfolio analytics capabilities as the platform scales.

In this role, you will:

 - Analyze U.S. power markets to identify market opportunities, portfolio risks, and drivers of performance

 - Partner directly with Portfolio Managers and Risk Managers to support portfolio construction, allocation decisions, and alpha research

 - Improve backtesting, experimentation, and simulation infrastructure to drive research outcomes

 - Develop quantitative models for risk analysis, scenario analysis, and performance attribution

 - Track and analyze portfolio P&L and exposures, delivering actionable insights to PMs and senior leadership

 - Support investment and risk decision-making under uncertainty by combining quantitative analysis and sound judgment

 - Collaborate closely with engineering, research, and leadership teams to scale Comity’s trading and risk platform

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.

 


WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE:

 - You have strong quantitative foundations in statistics, optimization, probability, machine learning, or applied mathematics

 - You have experience developing quantitative models for portfolio analytics, risk management, or trading applications

 - You have experience with performance attribution, portfolio optimization, or systematic trading analytics

 - You are comfortable influencing Portfolio Managers, Risk Managers, and senior stakeholders in fast-moving environments

 - You are a strong Python programmer with experience building analytical tooling and working with large datasets

 - You have strong intuition for markets, portfolio behavior, and risk under changing market conditions

 - You communicate quantitative insights clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - You are intellectually rigorous and operationally resilient; you dig into messy problems and drive them to resolution

Nice to have

 - Experience in U.S. wholesale electricity markets, including virtual trading, congestion modeling, nodal pricing, or FTRs

 - Advanced degree in a quantitative discipline such as mathematics, statistics, computer science, engineering, physics, or economics


LOCATION

We have hubs in New York City, Chicago, and San Francisco.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse ca

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
🎯 WHY WE EXIST

We’re on a mission to improve the reliability, transparency, and efficiency of our energy systems, fostering a future with sustainable and abundant energy. To accomplish our aims, we’re leveraging machine learning and optimization methods to price risk effectively for the financial rails of our future energy systems, and to accelerate the deployment of clean energy resources.

We envision energy systems that are efficient, autonomous, resilient, and powered by 100% renewable energy.




🧗 WHO WE ARE

Our founders (ex-Apple, Bluevine; ex-Affirm, Square, Google) are Stanford alumni with experience in large software systems, machine learning, and structured finance. Our world-class investors, Maverick Ventures https://www.maverickventures.com/ and Caffeinated Capital https://caffeinatedcapital.com/, are aligned to our policy objectives and platform vision.

We have hubs in Chicago, New York City, and San Francisco.




THE ROLE

Comity is looking for an Engineering Manager to lead our Platform Software Engineering team, which owns our research, market-making, and risk management platforms. You’ll lead the team that designs and scales the core data infrastructure powering all of these platforms, and works closely with researchers developing our price forecasting and grid optimization technology.

This role reports directly to the CTO and works closely with senior leadership across our Research and Business Operations teams. Given our team size, this is a hands-on role involving both senior IC work and people management. As both an early hire and team leader, you will have broad impact and ownership over our platform, technology, team practices, and culture.

Comity has two core teams: Power Markets and Commercial. This role is part of our Power Markets team.

 


WE’RE EXCITED ABOUT YOU BECAUSE:

 - You have 7+ years of professional experience as a software engineer, with 2+ years of experience as an engineering manager.

 - You are a hands-on leader who enjoys staying close to the code and day-to-day technical decisions of the team.

 - You have experience leading complex engineering projects in collaboration with cross-functional teams.

 - You are an excellent coder, system architect, and mentor of engineers of different seniority levels.

 - You are experienced and comfortable building and designing data systems with traditional relational databases as well as cloud-based data warehouses. Experience with machine learning concepts and technology is a plus.

 - You are a lifelong learner and empathetic teacher. We’re committed to the growth and development of our teammates. We work towards a shared understanding by listening with intent and holding open discussions because we know that's how we'll deliver quality results.

 - We believe strong foundations are more important than specific technical knowledge, but experience with any part of our stack (Python, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, AWS) is a plus.




LOCATION

This role will be based in one of our offices in Brooklyn, New York, or San Francisco, California.



At Comity, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. Our priority is to ensure that all applicants are provided with fair and equal access to employment opportunities. Recruiting and hiring decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other class protected by law.

notes: Energy-focused startup with strong AI/ML presence
